helper_landscape_summary: Two recent MoE eigenbasis routing papers (EMoE 2601.12137, ERMoE 2511.10971), Sinkhorn-style load-balancing router (2511.08972), and two classical Schur-Horn/majorization references. Candidate at intersection of spectral routing and majorization-constrained assignment.

hints.hint_text: Verify EMoE/ERMoE invoke Schur-Horn explicitly or only eigenbases.
Search 'doubly stochastic routing majorization' and 'OT expert balance' for Sinkhorn-routers implicitly enforcing majorization via Birkhoff polytope.
Map to router taxonomy: Expert-Choice, Soft-MoE, Sinkhorn, Spectral.
Classical Schur-Horn citation graph forward into ML?
Stripped = 'majorization-constrained assignment of diagonal to spectrum' is classical inverse problem.

solver_traces.reasoning: EMoE uses learned orthonormal eigenbasis for token projection but does NOT invoke Schur-Horn or majorization as a polytope constraint. The functional gap is the majorization polytope projection step.

solver_traces.search_query: doubly stochastic routing majorization optimal transport expert
solver_traces.reasoning: Sinkhorn-based routers implicitly enforce Birkhoff doubly-stochastic, which is a STRICT SUBSET of the Schur-Horn majorization polytope (Birkhoff captures permutation-convex hull; Schur-Horn captures diagonal-to-spectrum hull). The candidate's polytope projection is technically more general but functionally adjacent.

solver_traces.search_query: spectral load balancing transformer experts joint
solver_traces.reasoning: No single paper fuses all three (eigenbasis routing + Schur-Horn constraint + capacity allocation). Union of EMoE+Sinkhorn already covers 2 of 3 facets.
Candidate slots into 'projected gating with capacity constraint' branch; closest is Expert-Choice + Soft-MoE hybrid.
Q1 2026 OpenReview surfaces no Schur-Horn MoE submission; lag risk plausible but undocumented.

solver_traces.search_query: Schur-Horn classical ML application citation forward
solver_traces.reasoning: No ML application of 2004.03906 or 1403.4917 in 2024+ forward citations.
Algorithmic core IS well-known classically (Chu-Diele assignment), and Sinkhorn approximates it. Composition with MoE routing is the novel part — but functionally adjacent to Sinkhorn-MoE.

tree_stream_rationale: H2 (anticipated, 0.72 Sinkhorn-MoE as Birkhoff subset of majorization polytope) and H7 (anticipated, 0.70 classical Schur-Horn algorithm + Sinkhorn-MoE adjacency) flip tree-stream to FAIL. Combined with step 10 mechanical FAIL (5 keyword hits, max sem 0.72 EMoE), Q-rubric 0.675 ANTICIPATED. Verdict aligned across all signals.
